title: TCP 3-Way Handshake
description: The 3-step process that establishes a TCP connection before any data is sent.
fullExplanation: Before TCP can transfer data, both sides must agree to communicate and synchronize their sequence numbers. Step 1 — SYN: Client sends a segment with SYN flag set and a random Initial Sequence Number (ISN). Step 2 — SYN-ACK: Server responds with SYN+ACK, acknowledges client's ISN+1, and sends its own random ISN. Step 3 — ACK: Client acknowledges server's ISN+1. Connection is now ESTABLISHED. The ISN is random to prevent TCP sequence prediction attacks.

steps.name: SYN
steps.description: Client initiates connection. Picks random Initial Sequence Number x. SYN flag signals connection request.

steps.name: SYN-ACK
steps.description: Server accepts. Picks its own random ISN y. Acknowledges client's ISN by sending x+1. Both SYN and ACK flags set.

steps.name: ACK
steps.description: Client acknowledges server's ISN. Connection is now ESTABLISHED. Data can flow.

steps.name: FIN
steps.description: Initiator signals it has no more data to send

steps.name: FIN
steps.description: Receiver signals it has no more data to send

steps.name: ACK
steps.description: Initiator acknowledges. Connection CLOSED after TIME_WAIT

keyFacts: ISN (Initial Sequence Number) is random — prevents sequence prediction attacks
SYN cookies: encode state in ISN to handle SYN floods without storing half-open connections
TCP Fast Open: send data in SYN packet for repeat connections (saves 1 RTT)
TIME_WAIT state: 2×MSL (Maximum Segment Lifetime = 2 minutes) after close
Half-open connection: SYN sent but no SYN-ACK received yet
